correlation.attack_narrative: The attack originated from 185.220.101.47, which conducted a brute force credential attack against FINSERV-DB01, ultimately compromising the account 'Administrator' after multiple failed authentication attempts. Following initial access, the threat actor escalated privileges on FINSERV-DB01, gaining administrative or root-level control over the compromised system. A base64-encoded PowerShell payload was executed on the host, delivering a backdoor and establishing persistent command-and-control communication with the attacker infrastructure. The threat actor moved laterally to FINSERV-DC01, deployed ransomware, and exfiltrated data to 91.108.56.130 before triggering mass file encryption.
containment_actions: BLOCK IP 185.220.101.47 on all perimeter firewalls and WAF rules immediately
BLOCK IP 203.45.67.89 on all perimeter firewalls and WAF rules immediately
BLOCK IP 91.108.56.130 on all perimeter firewalls and WAF rules immediately
ISOLATE host FINSERV-DB01 from network — disconnect from all VLANs
ISOLATE host FINSERV-DC01 from network — disconnect from all VLANs
DISABLE account 'krbtgt' and reset all associated credentials
DISABLE account 'mimikatz' and reset all associated credentials
DISABLE account 'svc_backup' and reset all associated credentials
RESET all Domain Administrator and service account passwords immediately
QUARANTINE file C:\Windows\System32\lsass.exe — submit to threat intelligence platform
DECODE and analyse base64 payload: SQBFAFgAIAAoAE4AZQB3AC0ATwBiAG...
ACTIVATE incident response retainer — engage forensic team for full disk imaging
RESTORE affected systems from last known clean backup after full forensic capture
SCAN entire network for persistence mechanisms: scheduled tasks, registry run keys, new services
REVOKE all active VPN and remote access sessions pending investigation
